generatedSentence: A lonely juggler with a vibrant personality performed his show to a small, often braindead viewership, hoping to escape his backwater circumstances. He dreamed of a glorious future, beyond the mass of mundane items in his desktop office where he occasionally glimpsed fairies in the pixelated screens. His life felt like a constant bargain, an extra effort to avoid the ordeals of his past, especially the memory of a monstrous gorilla he once saw, an inaccurate object of his fear. He was an energizing presence, despite the dirt under his fingernails and the wools he wore. He possessed an emerald necklace, a family heirloom. He yearned for upgrades. One day, a hacker collective known as the 'Millennials' used a high bandwidth warship to threaten the global marketplace, creating an inversion of power. Their utopic vision was a preposterous decoy for true anarchy, causing many to suffer. The landowners and referees of the old world found themselves in jeopardy. Our juggler, now a self-proclaimed tester of digital security, realized his brain was vital. He studied mathematics, understanding the intricate chromosome code. His mind raced, calculating the impact of their actions. He felt a deep sense of polarity, a need to choose sides. The whole world was adrift. He hoped to vanish their influence, displaying courage for all mammals. The juggler's mindset shifted; he became berserk with purpose. He climbed the stairway, moving vertically, his movements as fluid as a gibbon or uakari, an artic catfishes' agility. He navigated an umbilical assembly of rails and walkways, an incentive to reach the nerve center. He knew this was his reckoning, a pivotal moment of showmanship. With a focused laugh and a powerful jump, he evaded security, a platinum flash against the neon glow. He found the central faucet, the core of their network, ready for his move, knowing the odds were stacked against him like a cyclone's force. He was no longer just an obedient fairy, but a force. He joyfully began recycling the corrupted data, causing the system to eject the hackers' influence like a napkin from a table, clearing the ground. The inventors of this new era happily watched as the nefarious network was purged, an obituary for digital tyranny. Lastly, the ozone layer, once threatened, began to heal. Through effective teamwork, this singular, glorious glimpse of justice made the ethnic group of millennials (and even some narcolepsy sufferers) glorify the juggler's actions. He ensured no one was braindead from digital overstimulation, enjoying the beats of peace, and even found time for jogging, leaving painkillers behind like a forgotten orchid or orache. Roger, the world was safe.
panelSentences: A lonely juggler with a vibrant personality performed his show to a small, often braindead viewership, hoping to escape his backwater circumstances. He dreamed of a glorious future, beyond the mass of mundane items in his desktop office where he occasionally glimpsed fairies in the pixelated screens. His life felt like a constant bargain, an extra effort to avoid the ordeals of his past, especially the memory of a monstrous gorilla he once saw, an inaccurate object of his fear. He was an energizing presence, despite the dirt under his fingernails and the wools he wore. He possessed an emerald necklace, a family heirloom. He yearned for upgrades.

One day, a hacker collective known as the 'Millennials' used a high bandwidth warship to threaten the global marketplace, creating an inversion of power. Their utopic vision was a preposterous decoy for true anarchy, causing many to suffer. The landowners and referees of the old world found themselves in jeopardy. Our juggler, now a self-proclaimed tester of digital security, realized his brain was vital. He studied mathematics, understanding the intricate chromosome code. His mind raced, calculating the impact of their actions. He felt a deep sense of polarity, a need to choose sides. The whole world was adrift. He hoped to vanish their influence, displaying courage for all mammals.

The juggler's mindset shifted; he became berserk with purpose. He climbed the stairway, moving vertically, his movements as fluid as a gibbon or uakari, an artic catfishes' agility. He navigated an umbilical assembly of rails and walkways, an incentive to reach the nerve center. He knew this was his reckoning, a pivotal moment of showmanship. With a focused laugh and a powerful jump, he evaded security, a platinum flash against the neon glow. He found the central faucet, the core of their network, ready for his move, knowing the odds were stacked against him like a cyclone's force. He was no longer just an obedient fairy, but a force.

He joyfully began recycling the corrupted data, causing the system to eject the hackers' influence like a napkin from a table, clearing the ground. The inventors of this new era happily watched as the nefarious network was purged, an obituary for digital tyranny. Lastly, the ozone layer, once threatened, began to heal. Through effective teamwork, this singular, glorious glimpse of justice made the ethnic group of millennials (and even some narcolepsy sufferers) glorify the juggler's actions. He ensured no one was braindead from digital overstimulation, enjoying the beats of peace, and even found time for jogging, leaving painkillers behind like a forgotten orchid or orache. Roger, the world was safe.
rawPanelSentences: A lonely juggler with a vibrant personality performed his show to a small, often braindead viewership, hoping to escape his backwater circumstances. He dreamed of a glorious future, beyond the mass of mundane items in his desktop office where he occasionally glimpsed fairies in the pixelated screens. His life felt like a constant bargain, an extra effort to avoid the ordeals of his past, especially the memory of a monstrous gorilla he once saw, an inaccurate object of his fear. He was an energizing presence, despite the dirt under his fingernails and the wools he wore. He possessed an emerald necklace, a family heirloom. He yearned for upgrades.

One day, a hacker collective known as the 'Millennials' used a high bandwidth warship to threaten the global marketplace, creating an inversion of power. Their utopic vision was a preposterous decoy for true anarchy, causing many to suffer. The landowners and referees of the old world found themselves in jeopardy. Our juggler, now a self-proclaimed tester of digital security, realized his brain was vital. He studied mathematics, understanding the intricate chromosome code. His mind raced, calculating the impact of their actions. He felt a deep sense of polarity, a need to choose sides. The whole world was adrift. He hoped to vanish their influence, displaying courage for all mammals.

The juggler's mindset shifted; he became berserk with purpose. He climbed the stairway, moving vertically, his movements as fluid as a gibbon or uakari, an artic catfishes' agility. He navigated an umbilical assembly of rails and walkways, an incentive to reach the nerve center. He knew this was his reckoning, a pivotal moment of showmanship. With a focused laugh and a powerful jump, he evaded security, a platinum flash against the neon glow. He found the central faucet, the core of their network, ready for his move, knowing the odds were stacked against him like a cyclone's force. He was no longer just an obedient fairy, but a force.

He joyfully began recycling the corrupted data, causing the system to eject the hackers' influence like a napkin from a table, clearing the ground. The inventors of this new era happily watched as the nefarious network was purged, an obituary for digital tyranny. Lastly, the ozone layer, once threatened, began to heal. Through effective teamwork, this singular, glorious glimpse of justice made the ethnic group of millennials (and even some narcolepsy sufferers) glorify the juggler's actions. He ensured no one was braindead from digital overstimulation, enjoying the beats of peace, and even found time for jogging, leaving painkillers behind like a forgotten orchid or orache. Roger, the world was safe.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: a lean figure wearing a patchwork leather trench coat and glowing emerald goggles. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist juggles glowing data orbs in a dimly lit, futuristic backwater alley, a single neon sign flickers overhead, full shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist confronts a holographic projection of a menacing warship, surrounded by complex digital interfaces, medium shot, dramatic lighting. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist scales a tangled, vertical 'umbilical' network of glowing rails and walkways in a vast, dark server room, dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist stands triumphantly amidst swirling, reforming data, a brilliant light emanating from their chest, the world outside visible and bright through a window, wide shot.

definitions.definition: (physical) Matter, material.
A large quantity; a sum.
To form or collect into a mass; to form into a collective body; to bring together into masses; to assemble.
To have a certain mass.
definitions.example: I mass 70 kilograms.

definitions.definition: Involving a mass of things; concerning a large quantity or number.
definitions.example: There is evidence of mass extinctions in the distant past.

definitions.definition: Involving a mass of people; of, for, or by the masses.
definitions.example: Mass unemployment resulted from the financial collapse.

definitions.definition: The chemical element with atomic number 78 and symbol Pt; a dense, malleable, ductile, highly unreactive, silverish-white transition metal of great value.
A whitish grey colour, like that of the metal.
A single or album that has achieved platinum sales, i.e. over 1 million or 2 million.
To reach platinum level in a game
definitions.example: I platinumed in Clash of Clans yesterday.

definitions.definition: Of a whitish grey colour, like that of the metal.
Of a musical recording that has sold over one million copies (for singles), or two million (for albums).
A set of non-physical psychological and social qualities that make a person (or thing) distinct from another.
definitions.example: The president has a unique personality.

definitions.definition: An assumed role or manner of behavior.
definitions.example: In his final act, the comedian takes on a child's personality.

definitions.definition: A celebrity.
definitions.example: Johnny Carson was a respected television personality.

definitions.definition: Charisma, or qualities that make a person stand out from the crowd.
definitions.example: The best contestant shows most personality.

definitions.definition: Something said or written which refers to the person, conduct, etc., of some individual, especially something of a disparaging or offensive nature; personal remarks.
definitions.example: indulgence in personalities

definitions.definition: That quality of a law which concerns the condition, state, and capacity of persons.
One of the tiny dots that make up the representation of an image in a computer's memory.
One of the squares that make up a pixel art work or a zoomed in image in a computer.
To create computer graphics by editing individual pixels.

An agreement between parties concerning the sale of property; or a contract by which one party binds himself to transfer the right to some property for a consideration, and the other party binds himself to receive the property and pay the consideration.
An agreement or stipulation; mutual pledge.
An item purchased for significantly less than the usual, or recommended, price
A gainful transaction; an advantageous purchase.
definitions.example: At that price, it's not just a bargain, it's a steal.

definitions.definition: The thing stipulated or purchased.
To make a bargain; to make a deal or contract for the exchange of property or services; to negotiate
definitions.example: They had to bargain for a few minutes to get a decent price for the rug.

definitions.definition: To transfer for a consideration; to barter; to trade
Having an irreversible loss of brain function and cessation of brain activity.
Having no useful thoughts; stupid; ditzy.
The control center of the central nervous system of an animal located in the skull which is responsible for perception, cognition, attention, memory, emotion, and action.
An intelligent person.
definitions.example: She was a total brain.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) Intellect.
definitions.example: She has a lot of brains.

definitions.definition: By analogy with a human brain, the part of a machine or computer that performs calculations.
definitions.example: The computer's brain is capable of millions of calculations a second.

definitions.definition: Oral sex.
Mind.
definitions.example: I have too much on my brain today.

definitions.definition: To dash out the brains of; to kill by smashing the skull.
To strike (someone) on the head.
To destroy; to put an end to.
To conceive in the mind; to understand.
Any of various green gemstones, especially a green transparent form of beryl, highly valued as a precious stone.
Emerald green, a colour.
Any hummingbird in the genera Chlorostilbon and Elvira; and some in the genus Amazilia
A size of type between nonpareil and minion, standardized as 6½-point.
To ornament with, or as if with, emeralds; to make green.
Of a rich green colour.
Agent noun of juggle; one who either literally juggles objects, or figuratively juggles tasks.
A person who practices juggling.
A person who performs tricks using sleight of hand, a conjurer, prestidigitator.
A magician or wizard.
A play, dance, or other entertainment.
An exhibition of items.
definitions.example: art show;  dog show

definitions.definition: A broadcast program/programme.
definitions.example: radio show;  television show

definitions.definition: A movie.
definitions.example: Let's catch a show.

definitions.definition: An agricultural show.
definitions.example: I'm taking the kids to the show on Tuesday.

definitions.definition: A project or presentation.
definitions.example: Let's get on with the show.   Let's get this show on the road.   They went on an international road show to sell the shares to investors.   It was Apple's usual dog and pony show.

definitions.definition: A demonstration.
definitions.example: show of force

definitions.definition: Mere display or pomp with no substance. (Usually seen in the phrases "all show" and "for show".)
definitions.example: The dog sounds ferocious but it's all show.

definitions.definition: Outward appearance; wileful or deceptive appearance.
(with "the") The major leagues.
definitions.example: He played AA ball for years, but never made it to the show.

definitions.definition: A pale blue flame at the top of a candle flame, indicating the presence of firedamp.
Pretence.
Sign, token, or indication.
Semblance; likeness; appearance.
Plausibility.
A discharge, from the vagina, of mucus streaked with blood, occurring a short time before labor.
To display, to have somebody see (something).
definitions.example: All he had to show for four years of attendance at college was a framed piece of paper.

definitions.definition: To bestow; to confer.
To indicate (a fact) to be true; to demonstrate.
To guide or escort.
definitions.example: Could you please show him on his way. He has overstayed his welcome.

definitions.definition: To be visible; to be seen; to appear.
definitions.example: At length, his gloom showed.

definitions.definition: To put in an appearance; show up.
definitions.example: We waited for an hour, but they never showed.

definitions.definition: To have an enlarged belly and thus be recognizable as pregnant.
(racing) To finish third, especially of horses or dogs.
definitions.example: In the third race: Aces Up won, paying eight dollars; Blarney Stone placed, paying three dollars; and Cinnamon showed, paying five dollars.

definitions.definition: To have a certain appearance, such as well or ill, fit or unfit; to become or suit; to appear.
Hideous or frightful.
Enormously large.
definitions.example: a monstrous height

definitions.definition: Freakish or grotesque.
Of, or relating to a mythical monster; full of monsters.
Marvellous; strange.
To break down and reuse component materials.
definitions.example: Both paper and plastic can be recycled.

definitions.definition: To reuse as a whole.
To collect or place in a bin for recycling.
To be recycled.
definitions.example: Sulfur recycles in the sulfur cycle.

definitions.definition: To discard into a recycling bin.
The practice of sorting and collecting waste materials for new use.
Those materials culled for recycling.
definitions.example: He plans to haul the recycling in on Saturday.

definitions.definition: A crazed Norse warrior who fought in a frenzy; a berserker.
Injuriously, maniacally, or furiously violent or out of control.
definitions.example: After seeing his sister stabbed to death, he went berserk and attacked the killer like a wild animal.

definitions.definition: Weird; bizarre.
The meat from a cow, bull or other bovine.
definitions.example: I love eating beef.

definitions.definition: (plural: beefs) A grudge; dislike (of something or someone); lack of faith or trust (in something or someone); a reason for a dislike or grudge. (often + with)
definitions.example: He's got a beef with everyone in the room.

definitions.definition: To complain.
To add weight or strength to; to beef up.
To fart; break wind.
definitions.example: Ugh, who just beefed in here?

definitions.definition: To feud or hold a grudge against.
definitions.example: Those two are beefing right now - best you stay out of it for now.

definitions.definition: To cry
definitions.example: David was beefing last night after Ruth told him off

definitions.definition: To fail or mess up.
definitions.example: I beefed my presentation hard yesterday.

definitions.definition: Being a bovine animal that is being raised for its meat.
definitions.example: We bought three beef calves this morning.

definitions.definition: Producing or known for raising lots of beef.
definitions.example: beef country

definitions.definition: Consisting of or containing beef as an ingredient.
definitions.example: beef stew

definitions.definition: The realm of faerie; enchantment, illusion.

A mythical being with magical powers, known in many sizes and descriptions, although often depicted in modern illustrations only as a small sprite with gauze-like wings, and revered in some modern forms of paganism.
An enchantress, or creature of overpowering charm.
A male homosexual, especially one who is effeminate.
A member of two species of hummingbird in the genus Heliothryx.
Like a fairy; fanciful, whimsical, delicate.
An amorous composition or poem.
Relating to or tending to arouse sexual desire or excitement.
definitions.example: Foreplay can get your erotic juices flowing.

definitions.definition: To undergo hardship.
definitions.example: Is anyone here afraid of suffering hardship?

definitions.definition: To feel pain.
definitions.example: At least he didn't suffer when he died in the car crash.

definitions.definition: To become worse.
definitions.example: If you keep partying like this, your school-work will suffer.

definitions.definition: To endure, undergo.
definitions.example: I've been suffering your insults for years.

definitions.definition: To allow.
A thing that has physical existence.
Objective; the goal, end or purpose of something.
(grammar) The noun phrase which is an internal complement of a verb phrase or a prepositional phrase. In a verb phrase with a transitive action verb, it is typically the receiver of the action.
A person or thing toward which an emotion is directed.
definitions.example: Mary Jane had been the object of Peter's affection for years.

definitions.definition: An instantiation of a class or structure.
An element within a category upon which functions operate. Thus, a category consists of a set of element objects and the functions that operate on them.
Sight; show; appearance; aspect.
To disagree with or oppose something or someone; (especially in a Court of Law) to raise an objection.
definitions.example: I object to the proposal to build a new airport terminal.

definitions.definition: To offer in opposition as a criminal charge or by way of accusation or reproach; to adduce as an objection or adverse reason.
To set before or against; to bring into opposition; to oppose.
The hair of the sheep, llama and some other ruminants.
A cloth or yarn made from the wool of sheep.
Anything with a texture like that of wool.
A fine fiber obtained from the leaves of certain trees, such as firs and pines.
Short, thick hair, especially when crisped or curled.
Yarn (including that which is made from synthetic fibers.)
Derogatory term for residents of the satellite towns outside Liverpool, such as St Helens or Warrington. See also Yonner.
A linear arrangement of condensed DNA and associated proteins (such as chaperone proteins) which contains the genetic material (genome) of an organism.
definitions.example: Chromosomes store genetic information.

definitions.definition: Something additional, such as an item above and beyond the ordinary school curriculum, or added to the usual charge on a bill.
An extra edition of a newspaper, which is printed outside of the normal printing cycle.
definitions.example: Extra, extra! Read all about it!

definitions.definition: A run scored without the ball having hit the striker's bat - a wide, bye, leg bye or no ball.
A supernumerary or walk-on in a film or play.
Something of an extra quality or grade.
Beyond what is due, usual, expected, or necessary; extraneous; additional; supernumerary.
definitions.example: I don't mind doing some extra work, as long as I get extra pay.

definitions.definition: Extraordinarily good; superior.
Over the top; going beyond what is normal or appropriate, often in a dramatic manner.
definitions.example: You unfollowed her for posting cat memes? You're so extra!

definitions.definition: To an extraordinary degree.
definitions.example: That day he ran to school extra fast.

definitions.definition: One who is expert at programming and solving problems with a computer.
One who uses a computer to gain unauthorized access to data, or to carry out malicious attacks.
A computer security professional.
Something that hacks; a tool or device for hacking.
A fork-shaped tool used to harvest root vegetables.
Someone who hacks.
One who is inexperienced or unskilled at a particular activity, especially a sport such as golf or tennis.
definitions.example: a tennis hacker

definitions.definition: One who operates a taxicab
The brief terminal part of a vowel or vocal element, differing more or less in quality from the main part.
definitions.example: a as in ale ordinarily ends with a vanish of i as in ill.

definitions.definition: A magic trick in which something seems to disappear.
definitions.example: The French drop is a well-known vanish involving sleight of hand.

definitions.definition: To become invisible or to move out of view unnoticed.
To become equal to zero.
definitions.example: The function f(x)=x^2 vanishes at x=0.

definitions.definition: To disappear; to kidnap
Collectively, the viewers of a television program or other video broadcast
An ethnic person, especially a foreigner or member of an immigrant community.
An ethnic minority.
A heathen, a pagan.
(in classical scholarship) the demonym of an Ancient Greek city
Of or relating to a group of people having common racial, ancestral, national, religious or cultural origins.
definitions.example: There are many ethnic Indonesians in the Netherlands

definitions.definition: Characteristic of a foreign, usually non-Western culture.
definitions.example: I like to eat ethnic food

definitions.definition: Representative of a folk or traditional mode of expression.
Heathen, not Jewish, Christian, or Muslim.
To invigorate; to make energetic.
To supply with energy, especially electricity; to turn on power to (something).
definitions.example: Whenever we energize that circuit we blow a fuse.

definitions.definition: To use strength in action; to act or operate with force or vigor; to act in producing an effect.
A rating.
definitions.example: I gave him a good grade for effort.

definitions.definition: The performance of an individual or group on an examination or test, expressed by a number, letter, or other symbol; a score.
definitions.example: He got a good grade on the test.

definitions.definition: A degree or level of something; a position within a scale; a degree of quality.
A slope (up or down) of a roadway or other passage
definitions.example: The grade of this hill is more than 5 percent.

definitions.definition: A level of primary and secondary education.
definitions.example: Clancy is entering the fifth grade this year.

definitions.definition: A student of a particular grade (used with the grade level).
definitions.example: The grade fives are on a field trip.

definitions.definition: An area that has been flattened by a grader (construction machine).
The level of the ground.
definitions.example: This material absorbs moisture and is probably not a good choice for use below grade.

definitions.definition: A gradian.
In a linear system of divisors on an n-dimensional variety, the number of free intersection points of n generic divisors.
A harsh scraping or cutting; a grating.
A taxon united by a level of morphological or physiological complexity that is not a clade.
The degree of malignity of a tumor expressed on a scale.
To assign scores to the components of an academic test.
To assign a score to overall academic performance.
To organize in grades.
definitions.example: a graded reader

definitions.definition: To flatten, level, or smooth a large surface.
To remove or trim part of a seam allowance from a finished seam so as to reduce bulk and make the finished piece more even when turned right side out.
To pass imperceptibly from one grade into another.
A relative of grasshoppers and crickets, in the family Tettigoniidae.
A painful or trying experience.
A trial in which the accused was subjected to a dangerous test (such as ducking in water), divine authority deciding the guilt of the accused.
The poisonous ordeal bean or Calabar bean
An umpire or judge; an official who makes sure the rules are followed during a game.
definitions.example: The referee kicked Jim out of the game for fighting.

definitions.definition: A person who settles a dispute.
A person who writes a letter of reference or provides a reference by phone call for someone.
definitions.example: Your application, along with letters from three referees, should be received by January 31.

definitions.definition: An expert who judges the manuscript of an article or book to decide if it should be published.
A distinct physical object.
definitions.example: Tweezers are great for manipulating small items.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An object that can be picked up for later use.
A line of text having a legal or other meaning; a separate particular in an account.
definitions.example: In response to the first item, we deny all wrongdoing.

definitions.definition: (psychometrics) A question on a test, which may include its answers.
definitions.example: The exam has 100 items, each of which includes a correct response and three distractors.

definitions.definition: A matter for discussion in an agenda.
definitions.example: The first item for discussion is the budget for next year's picnic.

definitions.definition: Two people who are having a relationship with each other.
definitions.example: Jack and Jill are an item.

definitions.definition: A short article in a newspaper.
definitions.example: an item concerning the weather

definitions.definition: A hint; an innuendo.
To make a note of.
The width, usually measured in hertz, of a frequency band.
Of a signal, the width of the smallest frequency band within which the signal can fit.
The rate of data flow in digital networks typically measured in bits per second; the bitrate.
The capacity, energy or time required.
definitions.example: I think it's a worthy project, but I just don't have the bandwidth right now.

definitions.definition: Any of several arboreal monkeys, of the genus Cacajao, from the Amazon basin.
That which attends, or relates to, or in some way affects, a fact or event; an attendant thing or state of things.
An event; a fact; a particular incident.
Circumlocution; detail.
Condition in regard to worldly estate; state of property; situation; surroundings.
To compel (a person or persons) to leave.
definitions.example: Andrew was ejected from his apartment for not paying the rent.

definitions.definition: To throw out or remove forcefully.
definitions.example: In other news, a Montreal man was ejected from his car when he was involved in an accident.

definitions.definition: To compel (a sports player) to leave the field because of inappropriate behaviour.
To project oneself from an aircraft.
definitions.example: The pilot lost control of the plane and had to eject.

definitions.definition: To cause (something) to come out of a machine.
definitions.example: Press that button to eject the video tape.

definitions.definition: To come out of a machine.
definitions.example: I can't get this cassette to  eject.

definitions.definition: A serviette; a (usually rectangular) piece of cloth or paper used at the table for wiping the mouth and hands for cleanliness while eating.
A nappy (UK), a diaper (American).
A small scarf worn on the head by Christian women (chiefly Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox) when entering a church, as a token of modesty.
A sanitary napkin.
The surface of the Earth, as opposed to the sky or water or underground.
definitions.example: Look, I found a ten dollar bill on the ground!

definitions.definition: Terrain.
Soil, earth.
definitions.example: The fox escaped from the hounds by going to ground.

definitions.definition: The bottom of a body of water.
Basis, foundation, groundwork, legwork.
(chiefly in the plural) Reason, (epistemic) justification, cause.
definitions.example: He could not come on grounds of health, or on health grounds.

definitions.definition: Background, context, framework, surroundings.
The area on which a battle is fought, particularly as referring to the area occupied by one side or the other. Often, according to the eventualities, "to give ground" or "to gain ground".
(by extension) Advantage given or gained in any contest; e.g. in football, chess, debate or academic discourse.
The plain surface upon which the figures of an artistic composition are set.
definitions.example: crimson flowers on a white ground

definitions.definition: In sculpture, a flat surface upon which figures are raised in relief.
In point lace, the net of small meshes upon which the embroidered pattern is applied.
definitions.example: Brussels ground

definitions.definition: In etching, a gummy substance spread over the surface of a metal to be etched, to prevent the acid from eating except where an opening is made by the needle.
(chiefly in the plural) One of the pieces of wood, flush with the plastering, to which mouldings etc. are attached.
definitions.example: Grounds are usually put up first and the plastering floated flush with them.

definitions.definition: A soccer stadium.
definitions.example: Manchester United's ground is known as Old Trafford.

definitions.definition: An electrical conductor connected to the earth, or a large conductor whose electrical potential is taken as zero (such as a steel chassis).

The area of grass on which a match is played (a cricket field); the entire arena in which it is played; the part of the field behind a batsman's popping crease where he can not be run out (hence to make one's ground).
A composition in which the bass, consisting of a few bars of independent notes, is continually repeated to a varying melody.
The tune on which descants are raised; the plain song.
The pit of a theatre.
To connect (an electrical conductor or device) to a ground.
To punish, especially a child or teenager, by forcing him/her to stay at home and/or give up certain privileges.
definitions.example: Eric, you are grounded until further notice for lying to us about where you were last night!

definitions.definition: To forbid (an aircraft or pilot) to fly.
definitions.example: Because of the bad weather, all flights were grounded.

definitions.definition: To give a basic education in a particular subject; to instruct in elements or first principles.
definitions.example: Jim was grounded in maths.

definitions.definition: To hit a ground ball. Compare fly (verb(regular)) and line (verb).
To place something on the ground.
To run aground; to strike the bottom and remain fixed.
definitions.example: The ship grounded on the bar.

definitions.definition: To found; to fix or set, as on a foundation, reason, or principle; to furnish a ground for; to fix firmly.
To cover with a ground, as a copper plate for etching, or as paper or other materials with a uniform tint as a preparation for ornament.
To improve or focus the mental or emotional state of.
definitions.example: I ground myself with meditation.

definitions.definition: (sequence) Used to mark the beginning of the last in a list of items or propositions.
definitions.example: Firstly, that's illegal; secondly, it has serious disadvantages; thirdly, it's not necessary; and lastly, did I mention that it's illegal?

definitions.definition: (discourse marker) Used to signal that the speaker is about to yield control of the conversation.
A set of steps allowing one to walk up or down.
definitions.example: The phrase "stairway to heaven" has a variety of cultural significances.

definitions.definition: An animal of the class Mammalia, characterized by being warm-blooded, having hair and feeding milk to its young.
A vertebrate with three bones in the inner ear and one in the jaw.
An abstract representational system used in the study of numbers, shapes, structure, change and the relationships between these concepts.
A person's ability to count, calculate, and use different systems of mathematics at differing levels.
definitions.example: My mathematics is always improving.

definitions.definition: By chance; perhaps.
By good chance; fortunately, successfully.
In a happy or cheerful manner; with happiness.
With good will; in all happiness; willingly.
One who obeys.
Willing to comply with the commands, orders, or instructions of those in authority.
definitions.example: Jessica was so intensely obedient of her parents that her brother sometimes thought she was a robot.

definitions.definition: To count; to enumerate; to number; also, to compute; to calculate.
To count as in a number, rank, or series; to estimate by rank or quality; to place by estimation; to account; to esteem; to repute.
To charge, attribute, or adjudge to one, as having a certain quality or value.
To conclude, as by an enumeration and balancing of chances; hence, to think; to suppose; -- followed by an objective clause
definitions.example: I reckon he won't try that again.

definitions.definition: To reckon with something or somebody or not, i.e to reckon without something or somebody: to take into account, deal with, consider or not, i.e. to misjudge, ignore, not take into account, not deal with, not consider or fail to consider; e.g. reckon without one's host
To make an enumeration or computation; to engage in numbering or computing.
To come to an accounting; to draw up or settle accounts; to examine and strike the balance of debt and credit; to adjust relations of desert or penalty.
The action of calculating or estimating something.
definitions.example: By that reckoning, it would take six weeks to go five miles.

definitions.definition: An opinion or judgement.
A summing up or appraisal.
The settlement of accounts, as between parties.
The working out of consequences or retribution for one's actions.
The bill (UK) or check (US), especially at an inn or tavern.
Rank or status.
(radio telecommunications) Received (used in radio communications to acknowledge that a message has been received and understood)
One who invents, either as a hobby or as an occupation.
The striking of one body against another; collision.
The force or energy of a collision of two objects.
definitions.example: The hatchet cut the wood on impact.

definitions.definition: A forced impinging.
definitions.example: His spine had an impingement; L4 and L5 made impact, which caused numbness in his leg.

definitions.definition: A significant or strong influence; an effect.
definitions.example: His friend's opinion had an impact on his decision.

definitions.definition: To collide or strike, the act of impinging.
definitions.example: When the hammer impacts the nail, it bends.

definitions.definition: To compress; to compact; to press into something or pack together.
definitions.example: The footprints of birds do not impact the soil in the way those of dinosaurs do.

definitions.definition: To influence; to affect; to have an impact on.
definitions.example: I can make the changes, but it will impact the schedule.

definitions.definition: To stamp or impress onto something.
definitions.example: Ideas impacted on the mind.

definitions.definition: The action of inverting.
Being upside down, in an inverted state.
Being in a reverse sequence, in an inverted state.
The move of one pitch in an interval up or down an octave.
The reversal of an interval.
The reversal of the pitch contour.
The reversal of a pitch class succession, such as a contrapuntal line or melody.

The subtraction of pitch classes in a set from twelve, which maps intervals onto their complements with respect to 0, and preserves interval classes, symbolized IX (X being the transposition that is inverted.).
A segment of DNA in the context of a chromosome that is reversed in orientation relative to a reference karyotype or genome

An increase of air temperature with increase in altitude (the ground being colder than the surrounding air). When an inversion exists, there are no convection currents and wind speeds are below 5 knots. The atmosphere is stable and normally is considered the most favorable state for ground release of chemical agents.

(grammar) Deviation from standard word order by putting the predicate before the subject. It takes place in questions with auxiliary verbs and in normal, affirmative clauses beginning with a negative particle, for the purpose of emphasis.
definitions.example: Inversion takes place in the sentence 'Is she here?' — 'is', the predicate, is before 'she', the subject.

definitions.definition: An operation on a group, analogous to negation.
Homosexuality, particularly in early psychoanalysis.
A set of pieces that work together in unison as a mechanism or device.
definitions.example: In order to change the bearing, you must first remove the gearbox assembly.

definitions.definition: The act of putting together a set of pieces, fragments, or elements.
definitions.example: assembly line

definitions.definition: A congregation of people in one place for a purpose.
definitions.example: freedom of assembly

definitions.definition: A legislative body.
A beat of the drum or sound of the bugle as a signal to troops to assemble.

In Microsoft .NET, a building block of an application, similar to a DLL, but containing both executable code and information normally found in a DLL's type library. The type library information in an assembly, called a manifest, describes public functions, data, classes, and version information.
A programming language in which the source code of programs is composed of mnemonic instructions, each of which corresponds directly to a machine instruction for a particular processor.
definitions.example: A skilled programmer can write very fast code in assembly language.

definitions.definition: A cord connecting an astronaut to a spacecraft, or a craft to ground control prior to launch, etc.
Of, or relating to, the navel (umbilicus) or the umbilical cord.
Such that the curvatures of normal sections are all equal to each other.
Soil or earth.
A stain or spot (on clothes etc); any foreign substance that worsens appearance.
Previously unknown facts, or the invented "facts", about a person.
definitions.example: The reporter uncovered the dirt on the businessman by going undercover.

definitions.definition: Meanness; sordidness.
In placer mining, earth, gravel, etc., before washing.
Freckles
To make foul or filthy; soil; befoul; dirty
The academic staff at schools, colleges, universities or not-for-profit research institutes, as opposed to the students or support staff.
A division of a university.
definitions.example: She transferred from the Faculty of Science to the Faculty of Medicine.

definitions.definition: An ability, skill, or power, often plural.
definitions.example: He lived until he reached the age of 90 with most of his faculties intact.

definitions.definition: A power, authority or privilege conferred by a higher authority.
(Church of England) A licence to make alterations to a church.
The members of a profession.
A soldier fit for duty
Having the power to produce a required effect or effects.
definitions.example: The pill is an effective method of birth control.

definitions.definition: Producing a decided or decisive effect.
definitions.example: The president delivered an effective speech!

definitions.definition: Efficient, serviceable, or operative, available for useful work.
definitions.example: How long does it take to make a bunch of civilians an effective military force?

definitions.definition: Actually in effect.
definitions.example: The curfew is effective at midnight.

definitions.definition: (of a cycle or divisor) Having no negative coefficients.
(for any effective theory) approximate; Not describing the fundamental dynamic changes in some system as they happen.
In the form of a cloud or haze; hazy.
Vague or ill-defined.
Relating to a nebula or nebulae.
An article of jewelry that is worn around the neck, most often made of a string of precious metal, pearls, gems, beads or shells, and sometimes having a pendant attached.
Anything resembling a necklace in shape.
definitions.example: a necklace of coral islands

definitions.definition: A device used in necklacing (an informal execution); a rubber tyre that is filled with petrol. It is placed around the victim's chest and arms, and set on fire.
To informally execute by necklacing; by setting on fire a petrol-filled rubber tyre which has been put around the bound victim's neck.
The largest of the apes, native to the forests of central Africa, and known for their trait of knuckle-walking.
A big and brutish man or a thug; a goon or ruffian.
A powerful person or organization; a heavyweight or behemoth.

A ruminant, of the genus Giraffa, of the African savannah with long legs and highly elongated neck, which make it the tallest living animal; yellow fur patterned with dark spots, often in the form of a network; and two or more short, skin-covered horns, so-called; strictly speaking the horn-like projections are ossicones.
A giraffe unicycle.
A laugh.
definitions.example: Are you having a giraffe?!

definitions.definition: A way of thinking; an attitude or opinion, especially a habitual one.
definitions.example: Earth Day is a way of propagating and celebrating the environmentalist mindset.

definitions.definition: A Japanese poem in three lines, the first and last consisting of five morae, and the second consisting of seven morae, usually with an emphasis on the season or a naturalistic theme.
A three-line poem in any language, with five syllables in the first and last lines and seven syllables in the second, usually with an emphasis on the season or a naturalistic theme.
definitions.example: Haiku, a poem

definitions.definition: A plant of the orchid family (Orchidaceae), bearing unusually-shaped flowers of beautiful colours.
A light bluish-red, violet-red or purple colour.
(colour) having a light purple colour.
In a vertical direction or position.
Something that motivates, rouses, or encourages.
definitions.example: I have no incentive to do housework right now.

definitions.definition: A bonus or reward, often monetary, to work harder.
definitions.example: Management offered the sales team a $500 incentive for each car sold.

definitions.definition: Inciting; encouraging or moving; rousing to action; stimulating.
Serving to kindle or set on fire.
An articulated lorry.
An articulated bus.
A person or object meant to lure somebody into danger.
A real or fake animal used by hunters to lure game.
To lead into danger by artifice; to lure into a net or snare; to entrap.
definitions.example: to decoy troops into an ambush; to decoy ducks into a net

definitions.definition: To act as, or use, a decoy.
The top surface of a desk.
A desktop computer.
definitions.example: Laptops are often more expensive than desktops.

definitions.definition: The main graphical user interface of an operating system, usually displaying icons, windows and background wallpaper.
definitions.example: I installed a new application and it added its icon to my desktop.

definitions.definition: Designed for use on a desk or similar piece of furniture.
Of an application, designed to be run on a personal computer.
A clearly defined path for pedestrians.
A small ape of the family Hylobatidae with long limbs, which it uses to travel through rainforests by swinging from branch to branch.
A person who owns land.
Danger of loss, harm, or failure.
definitions.example: The poor condition of the vehicle put its occupants in constant jeopardy.

definitions.definition: To jeopardize; to endanger.
An open area in a town housing a public market.
The space, actual or metaphorical, in which a market operates.
definitions.example: Some high-street retailers were slow to enter the new digital marketplace of the Internet.

definitions.definition: (by extension) The world of commerce and trade.
A place or sphere for the exchange of anything, such as ideas or fashions.
A demographic term for a person from the generation born from around the early 1980s to the mid 1990s or early 2000s; individuals who reached adulthood early in the 3rd millennium, C.E.
The state of being a north pole or south pole; the magnetic equivalent of electric charge
The separation, alignment or orientation of something into two opposed poles.
Either of the two extremes of such attributes.
The dipole-dipole intermolecular forces between the slightly positively-charged end of one molecule to the negative end of another or the same molecule. Wp
The division of an embryo into an animal pole and a vegetal pole within a blastula. Wp
Spatial differences in shape, structure, and function within a cell. Wp
A measure of the electrical potential at the ends of a circuit. Wp
The direction of a wave's amplitude or its being in phase or antiphase.
(grammar) The grammatical category of the affirmative and the negative. Wp
A duality that is an involution, i.e. has order two. Wp
An indicator of the positivity or negativity of a literal. Wp
Any of the various ways in which power is distributed within the international system. Wp
(gender) The concept of dualism between masculine and feminine. Wp
A correspondence between the points in the plane of a triangle not lying on the sides of the triangle and lines in the plane of the triangle not passing through the vertices of the triangle.
The saltbush: any of several plants, of the genus Atriplex, especially Atriplex hortensis or Atriplex patula, found in dry habitats, that have edible leaves resembling spinach.
Floating at random.
(of a seaman) Absent from his watch.
(often with of) Behind one's opponents, or below a required threshold in terms of score, number or position.
definitions.example: The team were six points adrift of their rivals.

definitions.definition: In a drifting condition; at the mercy of wind and waves.
The water held back by a dam or other obstruction
A remote place; somewhere that remains unaffected by new events, progresses, ideas, etc.
A rowing stroke in which the oar is pushed forward to stop the boat; see back water
To row or paddle a backwater stroke.
To vacillate on a long-held position.
A stroke; a blow.
A pulsation or throb.

definitions.definition: A pulse on the beat level, the metric level at which pulses are heard as the basic unit. Thus a beat is the basic time unit of a piece.
A rhythm.
The interference between two tones of almost equal frequency
(authorship) A short pause in a play, screenplay, or teleplay, for dramatic or comedic effect; a plot point or story development.
The route patrolled by a police officer or a guard.
(by extension) An area of a person's responsibility, especially
An act of reporting news or scientific results before a rival; a scoop.
That which beats, or surpasses, another or others.

definitions.definition: A precinct.
A place of habitual or frequent resort.
A low cheat or swindler.

definitions.definition: The instrumental portion of a piece of hip-hop music.
The act of scouring, or ranging over, a tract of land to rouse or drive out game; also, those so engaged, collectively.
A smart tap on the adversary's blade.
To hit; strike
definitions.example: As soon as she heard that her father had died, she went into a rage and beat the wall with her fists until her knuckles bled.

definitions.definition: To strike or pound repeatedly, usually in some sort of rhythm.
definitions.example: He danced hypnotically while she beat the atabaque.

definitions.definition: To strike repeatedly; to inflict repeated blows; to knock vigorously or loudly.
To move with pulsation or throbbing.
To win against; to defeat or overcome; to do better than, outdo, or excel (someone) in a particular, competitive event.
definitions.example: I just can't seem to beat the last level of this video game.

definitions.definition: To sail to windward using a series of alternate tacks across the wind.
To strike (water, foliage etc.) in order to drive out game; to travel through (a forest etc.) for hunting.
To mix food in a rapid fashion. Compare whip.
definitions.example: Beat the eggs and whip the cream.

definitions.definition: (In haggling for a price) of a buyer, to persuade the seller to reduce a price
definitions.example: He wanted $50 for it, but I managed to beat him down to $35.

definitions.definition: To indicate by beating or drumming.
definitions.example: to beat a retreat; to beat to quarters

definitions.definition: To tread, as a path.
To exercise severely; to perplex; to trouble.
To be in agitation or doubt.
To make a sound when struck.
definitions.example: The drums beat.

definitions.definition: To make a succession of strokes on a drum.
definitions.example: The drummers beat to call soldiers to their quarters.

definitions.definition: To sound with more or less rapid alternations of greater and less intensity, so as to produce a pulsating effect; said of instruments, tones, or vibrations, not perfectly in unison.
To arrive at a place before someone.

definitions.definition: To have sexual intercourse.

definitions.definition: To rob.
definitions.example: He beat me out of 12 bucks last night.

definitions.definition: A beatnik.
An upward grade or slope.
An improved component or replacement item, usually applied to technology

definitions.definition: An improvement
definitions.example: The remarried couples among my friends often joked that their divorce and remarriage amounted to an upgrade.

definitions.definition: To improve, usually applied to technology, generally by complete replacement of one or more components
definitions.example: When you upgrade your standard of living, you become addicted to that standard of living.

definitions.definition: To replace with something better.
definitions.example: I had to upgrade my anti-virus software to protect my computer from the newer threats.

definitions.definition: To improve the equipment or furnishings of or services rendered to
definitions.example: They upgraded him to the latest model.

definitions.definition: To improve in condition or status.
definitions.example: She upgraded to a more successful husband.

definitions.definition: To replace a program with a later version of itself, a version having a higher version number or marketed under a more recent product name.
Seeming to originate in utopia; utopian
Any ship built or armed for naval combat.
Any fish of the order Siluriformes, mainly found in fresh water, lacking scales, and having barbels like whiskers around the mouth
Someone who creates a fake profile on a social media platform in order to deceive people.
Such a fake profile.
An institution, building, or room for the exhibition and conservation of works of art.
An establishment that buys, sells, and displays works of art.
The uppermost seating area projecting from the rear or side walls of a theater, concert hall, or auditorium.
The, often elevated and in the rear, part of a courtroom where seating for the public audience is facilitated during trial.
A roofed promenade, especially one extending along the wall of a building and supported by arches or columns on the outer side
The spectators of an event, collectively.
A browsable collection of images, font styles, etc.

definitions.definition: (fortification) A covered passage cut through the earth or masonry.
A level or drive in a mine.
To show off
A long, slender ship propelled primarily by oars, whether having masts and sails or not; usually referring to rowed warships used in the Mediterranean from the 16th century until the modern era.
A light, open boat used on the Thames by customhouse officers, press gangs, and also for pleasure.
One of the small boats carried by a man-of-war.
The cookroom or kitchen and cooking apparatus of a vessel or aircraft; sometimes on merchant vessels called the caboose.
An oblong oven or muffle with a battery of retorts; a gallery furnace.
An oblong tray of wood or brass, with upright sides, for holding type which has been set, or is to be made up, etc.
A proof sheet taken from type while on a galley; a galley proof.
A representation of a single masted ship propelled by oars, with three flags and a basket.
A brief look, glance, or peek.

definitions.definition: A sudden flash.
A faint idea; an inkling.
To see or view briefly or incompletely.

definitions.definition: To appear by glimpses.
Exhibiting attributes, qualities, or acts that are worthy of or receive glory.
definitions.example: glorious deeds

definitions.definition: Excellent, wonderful
Bright or shining;
Eager for glory or distinction
Ecstatic; hilarious; elated with drink.
A canopy over a bed.
Something that overhangs something else; especially a canopy or soundboard over a pulpit.
A brief notice of a person’s death, as published in a newspaper.
A biography of a recently deceased person, written by a journalist and published in a newspaper.
A register of deaths in a monastery.
Relating to the death of a person.
An allotrope of oxygen (symbol O₃) having three atoms in the molecule instead of the usual two; it is a blue gas, generated from oxygen by electrical discharge.
Fresh air, especially that breathed at the seaside and smelling of seaweed.
To treat with ozone.
A horizontal bar extending between supports and used for support or as a barrier; a railing.
The metal bar that makes the track for a railroad.
A railroad; a railway, as a means of transportation.
definitions.example: We travelled to the seaside by rail.

definitions.definition: A horizontal piece of wood that serves to separate sections of a door or window.
One of the lengthwise edges of a surfboard.
A vertical section on one side of a web page.

definitions.definition: A large line (portion or serving of a powdery illegal drug).
To travel by railway.
To enclose with rails or a railing.
To range in a line.
To criticize severely.
Any of several birds in the family Rallidae.
To complain violently (against, about).
An item of clothing; a cloak or other garment; a dress.
Specifically, a woman's headscarf or neckerchief.
(of a liquid) To gush, flow.
Mistaken or incorrect; not accurate.
(broad sense) A weather phenomenon consisting of a system of winds rotating around a center of low atmospheric pressure
(narrow sense) Such weather phenomenon occurring in the South Pacific and Indian Ocean
A low pressure system.
The more or less violent, small-scale circulations such as tornadoes, waterspouts, and dust devils.
A strong wind.
A cyclone separator; the cylindrical vortex tube within such a separator
The realm of faerie; enchantment, illusion.

A mythical being with magical powers, known in many sizes and descriptions, although often depicted in modern illustrations only as a small sprite with gauze-like wings, and revered in some modern forms of paganism.
An enchantress, or creature of overpowering charm.
A male homosexual, especially one who is effeminate.
A member of two species of hummingbird in the genus Heliothryx.
An exposed plumbing fitting; a tap or spigot; a regulator for controlling the flow of a liquid from a reservoir.
(game development) One or several systems that inject currency into the game's economy, thus controlling or preventing inflation
A disorder characterized by sudden and uncontrollable attacks of deep sleep, often brief, sometimes accompanied by paralysis and hallucinations
definitions.example: Excitement-induced narcolepsy caused him to sleep through the most important events of his life.

definitions.definition: To exalt, or give glory or praise to (something or someone).
To make (something) appear to be more glorious than it is; regard something or someone as excellent baselessly.
To worship or extol.
An expression of mirth particular to the human species; the sound heard in laughing; laughter.
definitions.example: His deep laughs boomed through the room.

definitions.definition: Something that provokes mirth or scorn.
definitions.example: Your new hat's an absolute laugh, dude.

definitions.definition: A fun person.

To show mirth, satisfaction, or derision, by peculiar movement of the muscles of the face, particularly of the mouth, causing a lighting up of the face and eyes, and usually accompanied by the emission of explosive or chuckling sounds from the chest and throat; to indulge in laughter.
definitions.example: There were many laughing children running on the school grounds.

definitions.definition: To be or appear cheerful, pleasant, mirthful, lively, or brilliant; to sparkle; to sport.
(followed by "at") To make an object of laughter or ridicule; to make fun of; to deride; to mock.
definitions.example: Don't laugh at my new hat, man!

definitions.definition: To affect or influence by means of laughter or ridicule.
To express by, or utter with, laughter.
The act of jumping; a leap; a spring; a bound.
An effort; an attempt; a venture.
A dislocation in a stratum; a fault.
An abrupt interruption of level in a piece of brickwork or masonry.
An instance of propelling oneself upwards.
definitions.example: The boy took a skip and a jump down the lane.

definitions.definition: An object which causes one to jump, a ramp.
definitions.example: He went off a jump.

definitions.definition: An instance of causing oneself to fall from an elevated location.
definitions.example: There were a couple of jumps from the bridge.

definitions.definition: An instance of employing a parachute to leave an aircraft or elevated location.
definitions.example: She was terrified before the jump, but was thrilled to be skydiving.

definitions.definition: An instance of reacting to a sudden stimulus by jerking the body.
A jumping move in a board game.
definitions.example: the knight's jump in chess

definitions.definition: A button (of a joypad, joystick or similar device) used to make a video game character jump (propel itself upwards).

definitions.definition: (with on) An early start or an advantage.

An instance of faster-than-light travel, not observable from ordinary space.
A change of the path of execution to a different location.
Short for jump-start.

definitions.definition: To propel oneself rapidly upward, downward and/or in any horizontal direction such that momentum causes the body to become airborne.

definitions.definition: To cause oneself to leave an elevated location and fall downward.

definitions.definition: To increase sharply, to rise, to shoot up.

definitions.definition: To move to a position (in a queue/line) that is further forward.

definitions.definition: To attack suddenly and violently.

definitions.definition: To engage in sexual intercourse with (a person).
To cause to jump.

To increase speed aggressively and without warning.
To expose to danger; to risk; to hazard.

To thicken or enlarge by endwise blows; to upset.
(quarrying) To bore with a jumper.
To coincide; to agree; to accord; to tally; followed by with.

To flee; to make one's escape.
Exact; matched; fitting; precise.
Exactly; precisely
An occasion when a performer or team of them (especially in vaudeville) expects to perform at a theater for a single evening.

Either of the two partners involved in such a single sexual encounter.
The action of the verb to jog.

definitions.definition: The practice of running at a relatively slow pace for exercise.
Absurd, or contrary to common sense.
A drug that numbs the pain in the body.
